note: r4032login is config-only: a kernel 403 exception subscriber plus a settings config object and one alter event. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) probing documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site I want anonymous users who hit a 403 Access Denied page to be sent to the login form instead. Which module does this out of the box, and what setting controls where they are sent?
evals.expected_output: The r4032login module redirects anonymous users on 403 to the login page; the user_login_path setting (default /user/login) in r4032login.settings controls the target.

evals.prompt: With r4032login, how do I make a user return to the page they originally tried to access after they log in? Give the config key and its default.
evals.expected_output: The redirect_to_destination key (default true) on r4032login.settings appends the requested path as a destination query parameter so the user returns there after login.

evals.prompt: Which config object holds r4032login's settings, and which key sets the HTTP status code used for the redirect (and its default)?
evals.expected_output: All settings live in the r4032login.settings config object; default_redirect_code sets the status code, defaulting to 307 (options 307/302/301).

evals.prompt: In r4032login, how do I make already-authenticated users who lack access get a 404 Not Found instead of a 403? Which setting?
evals.expected_output: Enable throw_authenticated_404 (default false) on r4032login.settings; the subscriber then throws a CacheableNotFoundHttpException instead of redirecting authenticated users.

evals.prompt: How does r4032login intercept the 403 in Drupal 11 — what class and mechanism performs the redirect?
evals.expected_output: A kernel exception event subscriber, R4032LoginSubscriber (extends HttpExceptionSubscriberBase), whose on403() method handles the 403 exception and sets a redirect response.

evals.prompt: With r4032login I want to keep certain paths on the normal access-denied page instead of redirecting to login. Which settings control this?
evals.expected_output: match_noredirect_pages lists path patterns (one per line, * wildcard) to skip the redirect for; match_noredirect_negate (0/1) inverts it so only listed pages redirect.

evals.prompt: In a custom Drupal module I want to change the URL r4032login redirects to at request time. What extension point do I use?
evals.expected_output: Subscribe to the RedirectEvent (event name r4032login.redirect); use setUrl()/setOptions() to change the target URL or options before the redirect response is built.

evals.prompt: When should I use r4032login instead of relying on Drupal core's default access handling for a members-only section?
evals.expected_output: Core returns a bare 403 Access Denied page; use r4032login when you want anonymous visitors sent to the login form (and optionally returned to the page after login) for a friendlier experience, while core access control still decides who is denied.
